message:
test(fcp-conformance/enforcement-order): pin canonical 11-stage pipeline + CheckOutcome (br-96mui)

EnforcementCheckOrder::canonical_order is the NORMATIVE ordering every
FCP runtime MUST follow. The docstring is explicit: drift between
implementations would silently change which check fails first,
masking real failures behind incidental ones (e.g., a revoked
capability surfacing as a budget error). Zero conformance coverage
despite the explicit "MUST" pinning in source.

Adds 18 tests against the public fcp_core::enforcement API:

- canonical_order_returns_documented_eleven_check_sequence (the
  full sequence: CanonicalDecode -> ZoneMembership -> CapabilityVerify
  -> HolderProof -> CheckpointFreshness -> RevocationFreshness ->
  TaintApproval -> PolicyCeiling -> ConnectorManifest -> Budget ->
  RateLimit)
- count_constant_matches_canonical_order_length (COUNT == 11)
- index_of_agrees_with_canonical_order_position (every variant)
- runs_before_is_strict_less_than_on_indices (full 11x11 matrix)
- runs_before_is_irreflexive (no check runs before itself)
- as_str_matches_snake_case_wire_form_for_each_variant (11 literals)
- display_equals_as_str_for_every_variant
- json_serde_roundtrip_uses_snake_case (per-variant)
- canonical_order_is_deterministic_under_repeated_calls
- cheap_structural_checks_run_before_crypto_checks (decode/zone
  before capability_verify/holder_proof — design rule #1)
- crypto_checks_run_before_stateful_checks (capability/holder
  before budget/rate_limit — prevents budget enumeration via
  forged tokens)
- freshness_checks_precede_business_logic (revocation/checkpoint
  freshness before policy/budget — stale state can't be evaluated
  against unverified inputs)
- check_outcome_allow_is_only_for_allow_variant
- check_outcome_deny_is_only_for_deny_variant
- check_outcome_skip_is_neither_allow_nor_deny (Skip is the third
  variant — caller MUST handle it explicitly, not coerce to
  allow or deny)
- check_outcome_serde_roundtrip_for_all_variants
- check_outcome_uses_outcome_tag_in_json
- every_enforcement_check_id_appears_in_canonical_order_exactly_once
  (no duplicates, no missing — sanity check for index_of)

Mid-stream fix: HashSet not BTreeSet (EnforcementCheckId derives
Hash but not Ord).

All 18 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
enforcement_check_order_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
